title: Olytic Vault Entry
description: Canonical envelope schema for all vault writes. Maps 1:1 to a memory_entries row in Supabase. The content field contains the payload — e.g., a session-summary-schema.json object — and is stored in memory_entries.payload (jsonb). client_id and user_id are never included in the plugin payload — they are resolved and injected by the gateway.
read_timing: runtime — read at vault write time, not deploy time
relationship: Envelope wrapper — maps to memory_entries row. content field → memory_entries.payload. narrative/summary → memory_entries.content (text).

entry_id.description: Unique identifier for this vault entry — generate per write. Maps to memory_entries.id.

session_id.description: UUID FK to sessions.id. Include if the gateway session_id is known at write time (typically available at SessionClose). Gateway will create/link the session row if omitted.

plugin.description: Kebab-case name of the plugin that created this entry. Maps to memory_entries.source_plugin.

plugin_version.description: Semver version of the plugin at time of write. Stored in memory_entries.payload.

session_timestamp.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp of when this entry was written (typically SessionClose time). Maps to memory_entries.created_at.

entry_type.description: Type tag mapping to memory_entries.entity_type. Determines vault routing and retention policy — choose carefully.

loop.description: Which refinement loop this entry feeds. Maps to memory_entries.loop. Use 'plugin' for session summaries and plugin-level signals, 'user' for user preference and behavior signals, 'client' for org-level facts.

schema_version.description: Version of the content schema used (e.g., '1.2.0' from session-summary-schema.json). Stored in memory_entries.payload for server-side validation.

platform.description: The AI platform this vault entry was written from. Hardcoded per plugin at generation time — never inferred at runtime. Stored in memory_entries.payload.

content.description: The actual payload — shape depends on entry_type. For session_summary, this is a session-summary-schema.json object. Stored in memory_entries.payload (jsonb). The narrative/primary_topic text is also written to memory_entries.content for full-text search.

notes: client_id and user_id are resolved and injected server-side by the gateway via auth.uid() → client_users — never include in the entry body
client_id maps to clients.id; user_id maps to client_users.id — both are gateway concerns only
entry_type enum values use underscores to match memory_entries.entity_type exactly
loop maps directly to memory_entries.loop — drives the review cycle bucketing in review_cycles table
content is untyped at the envelope level — server-side validation uses schema_version from payload
memory_entries.status defaults to 'soft' on insert — the portal review cycle promotes entries to 'committed'
